segment-text: Based on the novel by Jenny Han
INT. COVEY HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - EVENING

LARA JEAN COVEY, 16, Korean-American lies sideways on an oversized arm chair reading a 1970's HARLEQUIN ROMANCE NOVEL. A DAMSEL IN DISTRESS is on the cover, a STRAPPING MAN looms either menacingly or sensually behind her. Lara Jean is ENRAPTURED with the book in a way that makes us think she's alone in the room.
My family says I live too much in head.
Something she reads in the book makes Lara Jean GASP. Then, a THROW PILLOW hits her in the side of the head.
(looking up)
On the other side of the room the pillow thrower aka Lara Jean's little sister KITTY, precocious, funny, doesn't realize she's only nine, sets up a game of SORRY!
Are you playing or what?
I just wanna finish this chapter.
Another sister, MARGOT, 18, very put together, an adult in a teenager's body, sits on the couch next to her boyfriend, JOSH, 17 - cute in a dorky way, typical guy-next-door.
It's okay, Kitty. We can play with
three people. From OC we hear a LOUD CRASH as a PAN DROPS in the kitchen.
Do you think we should go in there and help him?
You know he wants to do it himself.
I hate when Dad tries to make Korean food. It always tastes like a butt. Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
I don't where you got a taste for butt - but regardless - no matter what dad puts in front of you I want you to take a bite and say, "Mmmm. Just like Mom's." Okay?
I was still eating smushed peas and bananas when Mom died. I have no idea what her Bo-Ssam tasted like.
Come on, Kitty. Do it for dad.
Maybe if we didn't all pretend we loved his attempts at Korean food so much we wouldn't have to eat it on every special occasion. What's a girl gotta do to get a cheeseburger?
He doesn't want us to lose touch with our roots. It's sweet.
I love your dad's cooking.
Your opinion doesn't count. I once saw you eat a hot dog out of a trash can.
Is that true?
It was from the tray on top of a trash can and it was Lara Jean's leftovers. Not some stranger's.
Still. It'd crossed into the threshold of trash.
(to Lara Jean) Way to sell me out, by the way. Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
Lara Jean smiles, shrugs and goes back to her book.
We're ready!
The sisters give each other "here we go" looks.
INT. COVEY HOUSE - DINING ROOM - SAME

Everyone sits around the table. DR. COVEY, 50's, white, an OBGYN, trying his best to raise three daughters on his own, serves a VERY BURNT (and not in the right way) PORK SHOULDER. He struggles to cut it.
(whispers to Margot) It's suppose to fall off the bone. That's like a rubber chicken. Shh.
I ran out of time to make the kimchi so that's store bought, sorry girls.
The girls secretly smile. Dr. Covey passes a plate to Margot.
I can't believe this is your last night at home, kiddo. We're just gonna miss you so damn much.
Josh squeezes Margot's hand.
We sure are.
Margot politely pulls her hand away.
I can't believe we're not gonna see you until Thanksgiving.
Christmas, actually. Scotland is too far to come back for Thanksgiving.
Wait. Are you kidding? We won't see you til Christmas! That's so long! Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
(gentle ribbing) It'll fly by. Also with three months to practice maybe you'll be a passable driver by the time I get home.
Rude. I'm passable now. Ish.
Yeah, right. I'm gonna start wearing my bike helmet to school now that you're the one driving me.
(to Kitty) Not a bad idea. Hey!
Speaking of Thanksgiving - Thanksgiving was two topics ago. Now we're talking about what a bad driver Lara Jean is -
(pushing forward) I have a surprise.
He pulls a PRINT-OUT of an AIRLINE TICKET CONFIRMATION out of his pocket, hands it to Margot.
Since you can't come home. I thought I'd bring a little bit of home to you.
Margot stares at the print out. She's not saying anything.
It's a plane ticket! I'm coming to Scotland!!
Wow! So cool. Margot isn't that great?
You already paid for this? Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
Well, yeah. I booked it early so I could get a good rate.
You really should have talked to me before doing this.
I wanted it to be a surprise. What's the matter? You want me to come, don't you?
(intense) I. I don't know. You can't just spring this on me!
The family is shocked. No one is used to seeing Margot lose her cool. Josh is visibly wounded.
(softly) I guess I thought you'd be happy.
Awkward beat. Everyone silently stares at their plates. Lara Jean looks at Margot, trying to gauge what's going on. In an attempt to break the tension, Kitty takes a huge bite of pork and yells -
Mmmmm, just like Mom's!
As everyone turns to look at Kitty, Josh gets up from the table and leaves.
INT. LARA JEAN'S BEDROOM - LATER
Lara Jean stares out her window. She's spying on Margot and Josh who are arguing in the space between their two houses.

I guess I should back up and tell you a little about Josh. He lives next door to us and he and Margot have been a couple for the past two years. But before Margot even knew or cared that he existed. He was my first boy friend.
Blue (mm/dd/yyyy) EXT. NEIGHBORHOOD - DAY
Middle school versions of Lara Jean and Josh ride bikes around the neighborhood. Lara Jean has braces.
Space between the words. Boy who was a friend. We used to ride our bikes everywhere.
They jump over puddles and race down the street.
INT. MOVIE THEATER - EVENING
We liked all the same gory horror movies.
Josh and Lara Jean both scream and their popcorn goes flying into the air.
EXT. SCHOOL BLEACHERS - DAY

And we'd talk about everything. Even weird stuff. They eat their lunch on the empty bleachers at school LARA JEAN Would you rather only drink water for the rest of your life? Or, be allowed to drink anything you want but it all has a drop of pee in it?
Is it clean pee?
Yes. It's just one drop of clean, disease free pee.
(seriously considering) Clear or Yellow?
Gotta be at least a little yellow.
Pee. Definitely pee. (explaining) (MORE)
I don't think I can give up Mountain Dew. Plus side, it's yellow anyway so you wouldn't even notice.
Haha, Sicko.
Come on you know you choose pee too.
Oh yeah, definitely. Pee's the only way to go.
They both laugh.
It's not that we stopped being friends when Josh and Margot got together. It was just different.
INT. MARGOT'S CAR
Margot drives. Josh is in front and Lara Jean is in the back.
Margot had her license so we didn't need to ride our bikes anymore.
EXT. BLEACHERS -
Margot, Josh and Lara Jean eat their lunch on the bleachers.
And Josh was different too...
(grossed out) Water. Definitely water.
Yeah, me too. Definitely.
They didn't want me to feel left out so they invited me everywhere, even on dates.
Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
Lara Jean looks around the movie theater, all the other teenage couples are sucking face hard.
They tried to make it as normal as possible...
She looks over at Josh and Margot who, in contrast to the others, are very politely watching the screen.
But I still felt like a third wheel.
She looks down and sees they are HOLDING HANDS.
INT. LARA JEAN'S BEDROOM - LAST SUMMER
We see Lara Jean spying on Margot and Josh out her bedroom window. They are MAKING OUT.

It's not that I wasn't happy for them or wanted them to break-up or anything. But I missed my friend. And so...I did this thing I do, where when I'm trying to figure out what my feelings for someone, I write them a letter. (small beat) I guess you could call it a love letter.
She goes over to her desk and we see her writing, "DEAR JOSH..."
It wasn't a letter to send. It was just for me. To understand why I felt the way I did. How could you miss someone you saw all the time? And sometimes...
We see the words as she writes them: "I WONDER WHAT IT WOULD HAVE BEEN LIKE...
...I wondered what it would have been like if I'd realized how I felt about him sooner.
Blue (mm/dd/yyyy) INT. LARA JEAN'S BEDROOM

Lara Jean watches Josh and Margot from her window. They are standing in the same spot between their houses where we saw them making out in the flashback. But this time, they are definitely not making out. In fact, Josh might be crying.
But that's all ancient history.
Lara Jean walks over to her closet. She takes a LARGE, TEAL HAT BOX down from the top shelf. She opens it. We see FIVE ENVELOPES. She FLIPS THROUGH THEM.

I have five letters total. Each to a different boy. I keep them in a vintage hat box my mom gave me before she died. No one knows about them. But sometimes, alone in my room, I like to re-read them. The letters remind me how powerful my emotions can be. How all consuming. Margot would say I'm being dramatic. But I think drama can be fun. Just so long as no one else knows about it.
What are you doing?
Lara Jean jumps and hides the hat box in her closet. Nothing. Margot comes into Lara Jean's room, lays down on her bed. She looks exhausted.
Your room is a mess.
Lara Jean looks around. It is in fact a mess.
Are you okay?
Yes. No. I don't know. I just broke up with Josh.
You did what?? Why?
Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
Margot looks over to a framed photo of herself (age 10), Lara Jean (age 8) and Kitty (almost 3) with their MOTHER, EVE SONG COVEY, before she died.
Mom told me that you should never go to college with a boyfriend.
Lara Jean goes and gets in bed with Margot. Margot leans her head on Lara Jean's shoulder.
But you love Josh. I know.
So, do you think you might change your mind?
No. It's over.
Margot gets up. Like she's decided that's enough and they don't need to talk about her and Josh anymore.
When I was packing for college I made a pile of stuff I don't need anymore and asked Daddy to take it to Goodwill. You should make a pile too. It really is a mess in here.
I don't think there's anything I can really part with right now.
Margot picks up a BROKEN, PLASTIC POT HOLDER LOOM.
So you're currently using this broken pot-holder loom from fourth
I was thinking of getting into making wall hangings.
(all business) I'm leaving tomorrow. Lara Jean. That means you're gonna be the biggest sister. (MORE) Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
You need to set a good example for Kitty. (small beat) Tell you what? How bout I go get those boxes and help you? Fresh start to the year. (re: loom) We can start with this.
Margot exits with the loom. Lara Jean calls after her.
I was planning on making you a wall hanging for your dorm, you know!
(over her shoulder) I think I'll survive.
INT. COVEY HOUSE - FOYER - MORNING
We see a BIG PILE OF BOXES by the door marked GOODWILL. Next to two perfectly packed suitcases that say, MARGOT COLLEGE.
Margot says that when something is no longer useful, you either donate it, recycle it or throw it away.
EXT. COVEY HOUSE - SAME
Dr. Covey loads the boxes and Margot's suitcases into the trunk of his Volvo.
I always knew she felt that way about objects.
We see Josh watching the family from his window.
But I didn't think she could feel that way about a person too.
Lara Jean looks up at him. He disappears behind the curtain.
EXT. AIRPORT - ESTABLISHING - MORNING
We see Dr. Covey's Volvo pulls up in front of the DEPARTURES GATE.
Blue (mm/dd/yyyy) INT. AIRPORT - SAME
Dr. Covey and Kitty both hug Margot. Lara Jean hugs her last. Dr. Covey senses they need a moment.
Come on, Kitty. Let's go buy Margot a magazine for the plane.
They walk off.
Are you gonna be okay? School? Taking care of things around the house? Driving?
Don't worry about me. I got this.
Lara Jean smiles a BIG FAKE SMILE.
Do you really have this or are you doing that thing you do where you pretend you're okay when you're not.
You know about that?
You can lie to your father and you can lie to your friends. But you can't -
- lie to you sister. I know. It's super annoying.
Margot looks at Lara Jean. She hasn't answered her question.
I will be okay.
Do me a favor. Try and hang out with more than just Christine this year. Okay?
What are you talking about? Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
(not sure how to say it) Sometimes I worry you don't have enough people in your life.
That's crazy. I have tons of people.
Me, Daddy, Kitty, Christine. That's it.
And Josh.
Do you really think you'll still be hanging out with my ex-boyfriend after I leave for college?
I ate lunch with the two of you everyday last year. What am I supposed to do? Eat lunch alone.
That's what I'm talking about. Even if we add Josh that's still only what - six people? And I won't even be here.

(losing it a little) Exactly. You know what happens when you let people into your life. They either leave or they die. (small beat then softer) So excuse me for not wanting to put myself through more of that.
(quietly, an apology) I have to go to college, Lara Jean.
(what's really wrong) I know. It's just. It's gonna be so weird not having you around this year. Did you have to pick the farthest college you could possibly think of? Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
Hey. If you need me, I'm only a skype away.
Til you start going to pubs and eating haggis with your new Scottish friends and forget about us.
I can promise you...I will never eat haggis.
They both smile. They really love one another. Dr. Covey and Kitty come back holding a HUGE STACK OF MAGAZINE.
We couldn't decide so we got you
all of them.
INT. AIRPORT - LATER
Dr. Covey puts his arms around Lara Jean and Kitty as they watch Margot walk up the stairs to go through security, holding the huge pile of magazines.
Think she'll turn back around?? No.
They keep watching as she confidently shows her passport and ticket. She doesn't turn around.
That's not Margot.
EXT. COVEY HOUSE - THE NEXT DAY - MORNING
Dr. Covey takes a photo of Lara Jean and Kitty in front of the house.
Fourth grade and Junior year! I can't believe it. Smile!
The girls humor their father and smile.
You ready to go, Kitty?
Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
Kitty runs into the house and returns wearing her bike helmet.
Very funny.
Very necessary.
INT. ADLER HIGHSCHOOL - HALLWAYS

Lara Jean walks down the center of the hallway, alone. On either side of her we see students hugging, asking each other about their summers, comparing course schedules. Across the hall, Lara Jean sees Josh putting his books in his locker. She waves. He waves back. Both unsure what their relationship is supposed to look like now that Margot is gone. Blushing but not sure why, Lara Jean turns and not looking where she's going she walks directly into GEN, 16, blonde, intimidatingly pretty. The kind of girl who gets off on people being afraid of her.
Oh my god, Gen! I'm so sorry. I wasn't paying attention... Obviously.
Gen. Genevieve. Someone who treated me like an old pot holder loom and recycled our friendship as soon as we left middle school and I stopped being cool enough for her.
Genevieve holds her foot, pouting. Her boyfriend, PETER
(to Gen) Sup with your foot? (to Peter) I think she broke my toe with those witch shoes.
She looks down at Lara Jean's VINTAGE BOOTIES.
They're vintage. I found them on Etsy. They're ridiculous. CHRISTINE, 17, cool, over high school and doesn't give a shit
what anyone thinks of her, walks up. She puts her arm around Lara Jean.
Actually, they're amazing.
Christine. Chris. Gen's cousin. My best friend. My only friend, really.

(to Gen) But shoes so dope are really hard to pull off. Lara Jean can rock em, natch, but for you Cuz...Well, let's just say it's probably a good thing you're playing it safe with those Uggs. That was clearly not a compliment and Gen knows it. (to Peter) Do you smell that?

No. I don't smell anything. Blue (mm/dd/yyyy) You do. It's like, an old person foot smell. It's making me wanna (to Lara Jean re: shoes) You know you can catch toenail fungus from used shoes, right? Better be careful. Athlete's foot is no joke. Come on, Peter.
Peter and Gen walk off.
Ignore her. You can't catch toenail fungus from Etsy. And if anything
smelled it was his Axe Body Spray.
Thanks but be honest, do they really look like I got them at Halloween adventure?
Kind of. But in a hot way. Like
Kendall Jenner meets Morticia
That's what I think! Ugh. Your cousin is such a bitch.
And you're just realizing this now?
Lara Jean smiles. The bell rings.
INT. CAFETERIA - LUNCH TIME

Lara Jean walks into the cafeteria and looks around the room. Gen and Peter are holding court at their table. Last year she would have been sitting with Josh and Margot. She texts Chris. We see their exchange. CHRIS: SUBWAY!! SWEET ONION CHX TERIYAKI TIME YO! KYLE PICKED LJ: Who's Kyle?
Blue (mm/dd/yyyy) INT. LIBRARY - A FEW MINUTES LATER

Lara Jean walks into the library. She sees a group of students sitting at a long table silently eating their lunches and studying. She sits down, takes out her ROMANCE NOVEL and starts reading. A boy doing calculus eyes her book -
(judgemental) Yeah. I didn't think so.

Lara Jean sighs. Maybe she doesn't belong here either. She bites into a carrot. It crunches. All the studying students glare at her. Someone points to a sign that says: SOFT FOODS ONLY. She picks up her things and leaves.
EXT. BLEACHERS - SAME
Lara Jean sees Josh eating alone, reading a SCI-FI NOVEL on the empty bleachers. She timidly walks up to him.
This seat taken?
Yep. By you.
She sits down. Neither is really sure what they are supposed to talk about.
It's kind of weird without Margot, huh?
Yeah. A little.
Should we maybe just... (she eyes his book) ...read?
Josh and Lara Jean both pick up their books and read next to one-another. She silently passes him a carrot stick.
He takes it. It crunches. He passes her a chip, she takes it. It crunches. Whatever this is - it's working for now.
INT. LARA JEAN'S CAR - AFTERNOON
Lara Jean drives Kitty home from swim practice. Kitty sits in the front seat in her bathing suit and helmet. Lara Jean is very focused on the road. She grips the wheel like an eightyyear-old woman.
First day was good?
Great. Alison wanted to sit next to me at lunch but so did Brianna and so did Katy and Megan so I ended up rotating so everyone had time to sit next to me. You know?
Yeah. Totally know what's that's like. And how was swim practice?
That was amazing too. Did you see how fast I was going? Uh-huh. That was like Michael Phelps fast, right?
Um. Maybe not quite Phelps fast...
No. You didn't see the first match. I was totally Phelpsing it.
Lara Jean smiles at her little sister.
Sorry I missed that.
And I was definitely swimming faster than you're driving right now. You know it's illegal to go too slow right? Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
I'm not going too slow. I'm going like 5 miles below the speed limit. And can you take off that helmet? You're making me nervous.
I'll take off the helmet if you speed up.
Lara Jean speeds up but, distracted, she misses a stop sign. Before she knows what's happening she HITS A CAR in the intersection.
EXT. SUBURBAN INTERSECTION - SAME
An OLD MAN gets out of his BUICK.
(to Kitty, freaking out) Oh my god. Are you okay? Kitty is fine. She's laughing. Good thing I'm wearing the helmet!
Lara Jean runs up to the old man whose Buick she hit.
Sir. I am SO SORRY.
What the hell were you doing, catching pok-e-mon?? Didn't you see me there?
Should we call the police?
The po-po?! I can't have my whole day wrapped up in this crap. I'm calling numbers at the VFW in an hour. My car is fine. Be more careful!
The old man gets back in his car and speeds off.
Your contribution to the stereotype that Asian women are bad at driving is so not cool.
Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
You're not helping.
Lara Jean, are you okay?
Lara Jean looks up. Sees Peter.

Of course. At moments of highest embarrassment you always run into the people you want to see least. I failed to mention before that Peter Kavinsky, Gen's Peter, also happens to be the non-recipient of one of my love letters.
INT. PETER KAVINSKY'S BASEMENT - FLASHBACK
Peter, Lara Jean, Gen and some other seventh graders sit in a circle playing spin the bottle.
Seventh Grade. My first real boy/girl party.
Lara Jean picks up the bottle to spin it.
Everyone knew that Peter and Gen, who at this time was still my BFF, only wanted to kiss each other.
Lara Jean spins the bottle. It lands on Peter.
(to Gen) I can spin it again.
The group starts chanting: Kiss! Kiss! Kiss!
Can't cheat the bottle.

Peter gets up and kisses Lara Jean. Gen glares at them. Lara Jean blushes uncontrollably. She stares into Peter's eyes. Lara Jean is staring into Peter's eyes again, but this is the hot, seventeen year old Peter. The one who's Gen's boyfriend. The one who hasn't spoken to her in years. Everything is still fuzzy from the accident. Lara Jean blinks. Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
I said are you okay? Do you want me to call triple A for you?
Call triple A! Of course. Such an idiot.
Did you just call me an idiot?
Did I say that out loud? Yes.
I was talking to myself.
(whispered to Lara Jean) Jesus. Be cool woman.
So, do you want me to call them?
No. I can do it. Thanks.
You sure? You seem pretty shaken up. I could wait with you guys til they get here.
Kitty stares at Lara Jean and Peter, clocking their interaction.
(embarrassed ramblings) We're fine. Really. You should go. Move on down the road. Seriously.
Okay. But you know you need to move
the car out of the intersection, right? It's blocking traffic. Lara Jean looks terrified.
I have to get back in that thing??? PETER
You know what. Here He gets in her car and moves it to the side of the road. When he gets out, he tosses Kitty the keys.
(to Kitty) You're in charge.
Peter gets in his Audi and drives off.
Who was that?
INT. LARA JEAN'S BEDROOM - SATURDAY NIGHT
Lara Jean skypes with Margot.
You already wrecked the car?
The mechanic said it wasn't a bad accident, he just has to order a special part. Daddy wasn't even mad.
Still. Wow. (small beat) So. What else is going on? Who have you been eating lunch with?
(lying) Chris.
I thought Chris usually skipped lunch and got whatever random guy she's seeing to take her to subway.
I've been reading at lunch, okay?
Lara Jean! We talked about this. New Friends! Remember? Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
I know. I know.
Or see if Josh will eat with you. It's better than reading alone.
Thanks for the advice.
Lara Jean! It's starting!
Gotta go. Miss you.
Miss you too. Call me later.
INT. COVEY HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - SAME
Lara Jean and Kitty sit on the couch eating raw cookie dough and watching a Golden Girls marathon.
What do you think she's doing tonight? Crack.
Kitty! Why would you say that??
It's how Scottish people say fun. Like "you're good crack". I looked it up.
Oh. It's weird not having her here.
It's weird not having Josh here too. You don't think he'll stop coming over now, do you?
I mean probably. Why would he want to hang out with us now that Margot's gone?
Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
I miss his Blanch impression.
They eat cookie dough and look back at the screen.
Lara Jean, have you ever been in love?

(taken aback) Where'd that come from? I was just thinking that since Margot's gone and Josh isn't coming over as much and you're such a shitty driver...if you had a boyfriend, we'd still have someone to take us places. Like, what about that guy from the accident?
Lara Jean practically spits out her cookie dough.
Do you mean Peter Kavinsky? Yeah. Him.
He's dating Gen. So?
So, someone liking me over Gen would be like someone liking Rose over Blanche.
I've always preferred Rose. She's got hidden depths.
Look, Kitty. We don't need some guy to drive us around. As soon as the car is fixed I'm gonna get better at driving, okay? I promise.
It's not just that.
Kitty pauses. How's she gonna say this?
Don't you think it's kind of depressing that it's the first Saturday night of the new school year and you're spending it watching a Golden Girls marathon with your little sister?
No. I love the Golden Girls. And I love you.
Lara Jean tries to tickle Kitty. But Kitty is serious.

Okay, I'm not telling you this to make you feel bad but, Lara Jean, I'm nine and I cancelled plans to be here tonight. You're sixteen and well, I don't think you had anything else going on...am I right?
That's cold, Kitty. That's sups cold.
INT. COVEY HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - LATER
Lara Jean snores on the couch, totally passed out. Kitty pinches her nose to make sure she's really asleep. Convinced she's out, Kitty sneaks away.
INT. LARA JEAN'S BEDROOM - SAME
Kitty moves a chair over to Lara Jean's closet. She stands on it and takes something down, we can't see what.
This is for your own good.
EXT. ATHLETIC FIELDS - MONDAY MORNING
Christine and Lara Jean run laps during gym class.
What'd you do this weekend?
Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
I finished my book. It was so good.
Another bodice ripper, you little perv?
I enjoy them for their camp.
Pssh. Yeah right. Don't worry, girl. No Judgement.
Lara Jean rolls her eyes and smiles. The girls keep running. Peter Kavinsky runs up next to them. He stares at Lara Jean.
(to Lara Jean) We need to talk.
(confused) We do?
Hey, PK, I heard my cousin dumped you for a college dude, is that true?
Hey, Chris, I heard you have a tail. Is that true?
Yep. Curly cue. Like a little piggy.
Sick. I need to talk to Lara Jean. Alone.
Christine gives Lara Jean a look that says: What does Peter Kavinsky need to talk to you about? Lara Jean gives a look back that says: Damned if I know.
If you need me I'll be pretending to have cramps and reading magazines in the nurse's office.
Christine runs off the field. Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
(screaming back) I HAVE MY PERIOD!
The coach blushes as moves on.
Does she really have a tail?
No. Obviously not.
It's so weird to me that you guys are friends. You have like, nothing in common.
Is that what you came over here to talk to me about?
No. I want you to know that I'm flattered but things with Gen and me are still up in the air and well, sorry, but - it's not gonna happen.
(genuinely confused) I'm sorry. What?

Peter pulls LARA JEAN'S LOVE LETTER out of his pocket. As soon as she sees it she FAINTS on the field. PASSES OUT COLD. EXT. ATHLETIC FIELDS - A FEW MINUTES LATER PETER Lara Jean! Lara Jean. Are you okay? Lara Jean fuzzily comes to. She's laying on the field. Peter is crouching over her.
What just happened?
You just passed out.
Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
Lara Jean looks around. She sees that Josh is walking towards her. QUICK CUTS as we show three increasing CU shots of LARA JEAN's LOVE LETTER in Josh's hand.
OH. MY. GOD. What?

Josh is getting closer and closer. Lara Jean has no idea what to do. Without thinking she pulls Peter down onto the ground with her and starts furiously making out with him. The coach blows a whistle.
(to Lara Jean and Peter) Hey! Stop that! Don't make me pull the hose on you two!
Lara Jean looks up. Everyone on the field is watching her. Josh looks extremely confused. He looks down at his own letter then back at Lara Jean and Peter.
(confused but impressed) Damn. That was unexpected.
Lara Jean is dumbfounded. What the hell did she just do?!?!?
OH. MY. GOD.
Yeah. I'll say.
Ohmygod. Ohmygod. Ohmygod.

She jumps up and sprints off the field. She doesn't stop sprinting until she's in the INT. GIRL'S BATHROOM Lara Jean runs into a stall, slamming the door behind her. She's sweating. WTF is happening?? She presses her fingers into her temples, praying this is all a dream, knowing that it's not. She hears a small tap on the stall door. Blue (mm/dd/yyyy)
Lara Jean? Are you in there?
Lara Jean opens the door to see LUCAS, 16, black, too handsome for high school with the one exception that he's still a little scrawny. He holds a LOVE LETTER in his hand.
Oh god. Lucas James. Letter number three.
FLASHBACK INT. SCHOOL GYM - HOMECOMING - FRESHMAN YEAR

The school gym is decorated. Freshman versions of Gen and Peter dance in the midst of a huge crowd of people. Over by the punch bowl Lucas stands by himself, bopping his head to the music. He wears a CRAVAT.
Homecoming. Freshman year. Lucas and I both went alone and we both still had braces.
Freshman Lara Jean walks up to freshman Lucas. Both of their braces are OUT OF CONTROL. This is not invisaline. Hi Lucas.
Oh, hi Lara Jean. Who are you here with?
Well I drove with my sister and
